Defines memory layout and constants for flash regions in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This C header file defines constants related to the memory layout of a flash storage system. It specifies the sizes and address masks for sectors and subsectors, which are used to manage memory allocation and alignment. The file outlines various memory regions, such as those reserved for debug logs, manufacturing information, firmware scratch space, and system resources, each with defined start and end addresses. Additionally, it includes regions for testing the flash interface, with a note that data in these regions may be corrupted during tests. The file also contains conditional compilation checks to ensure that the flash test space is within the expected range and includes placeholder regions for application and migration purposes, which are not used in the current flash part.
